rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component 1,2-bromoesterification of alkenes with carboxylic acids and N-bromosuccinimide via TEMPO-mediated anodic oxidation forming a bromonium intermediate.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of TEMPO to TEMPO+ which activates NBS toward Br+ transfer to the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2.
electron_flow: TEMPO is oxidized at the anode to TEMPO+, which activates NBS to deliver Br+ to the alkene, generating a cyclic bromonium ion; carboxylic acid opens the bromonium and deprotonation gives the β-bromo ester, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Used as a 50 mol% redox mediator/initiator; oxidized at the anode to TEMPO+ which activates NBS.

notes: Mechanism is proposed without explicit radical trapping, CV, KIE, or DFT studies; TEMPO is invoked as a redox mediator rather than a radical trap. Pathway is cationic (bromonium) rather than radical, despite the electrochemical oxidative conditions.
